name: Jimmy Walker
abstract: James John Walker (June 19, 1881 – November 18, 1946), known colloquially as Jimmy Walker and Beau James, was an American attorney, lyricist, and Democratic Party politician who served as the 98th mayor of New York City from 1926 until his resignation in 1932. A flamboyant politician, he was a liberal Democrat and part of the powerful Ta…

works_created.t: Will You Love Me in December as You Do in May?

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: I used to play in this fusion band called Free Flight. A flute player named Jimmy Walker and Mike Garson the piano player, and Alex Acuna played drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Ernie Anderson went to the Ex-Mayor Walker, Jimmy Walker, who was the vice-president of the company, Majestic Records, and talked Jimmy into hiring me as a house band.

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Brownlee
about_quotes.evidence: He'd go around with Paul and all, partying at night with Jimmy Walker and that bunch up in New York.
